name: Preflight Verdict Schema + Fixtures (PR-F2, handover F-008)

design_notes.nature_of_this_feature: Handover F-008. Add schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json: the machine-readable JSON Schema for the clean-state preflight run's verdict artifact whose PROSE convention feature 033 already shipped in ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md Sec 6 ('added by a later feature in this phase; not yet present at the time this protocol was authored'). The protocol document is the semantic authority; this feature only encodes its declared shape as a validatable schema, plus positive and negative fixtures, plus indexing the new schema in NIZAM.json and schema/README.md. Per .agent/feature_list_005.json feature 038 acceptance core: (1) 'Three verdicts supported' -- verdict is a required string enum of exactly PASS / PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS / FAIL, no other value valid (docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md 'Preflight Verdict'; protocol Sec 3, 'exactly one ... No other value is a valid preflight verdict'); (2) 'Exceptions require structured approval state' -- an if/then JSON Schema conditional requires an 'operator_approval' object (approver identity, timestamp, verbatim authorization-or-reference) whenever verdict is PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS, per protocol Sec 5 (Operator-Exception Rule: 'the operator's decision (identity, timestamp, and the specific exceptions accepted) is recorded in the same verdict artifact ... never as a separate, unlinked record') -- never required for PASS or FAIL. This feature does NOT define the CLI that produces the artifact (041's scope), does NOT define the individual-finding schema an 'exceptions' array item should conform to (039's engineering_finding.schema.json; this schema's 'exceptions' items are deliberately open, additionalProperties:true, so it composes with 039 rather than pre-empting it), and does NOT re-author ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md's prose (033's scope, already shipped).
design_notes.schema_design: Top-level required key: 'verdict' (string enum, exactly ['PASS','PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS','FAIL']). Optional top-level keys, all additionalProperties-open per the house schema convention (schema/README.md 'Every schema in this module ... Permits additionalProperties on extension points'): 'execution_id' (matches the <execution-id> path segment of the artifact's own location, protocol Sec 6), 'generated_at', 'exceptions' (array of open-shape finding objects), 'operator_approval' (object). CRITICAL, MADE EXPLICIT PER .agent/qa/038-contract-review.json's HIGH finding (revision 0 -> 1): 'operator_approval' itself declares its OWN internal 'required': ['approver','approved_at','authorization'] array (not merely referenced-by-name in prose, and not merely relying on the outer allOf/if/then's coarser 'required':['operator_approval']) -- so operator_approval:{} (the key present but empty) is rejected on its own terms regardless of the outer conditional -- and each of the three sub-fields is typed 'string' with 'minLength':1, so an empty-string sub-field value is separately rejected too. A single allOf/if/then entry makes BOTH 'operator_approval' (with its own internal required array applying) AND a non-empty 'exceptions' array REQUIRED exactly when verdict's const is PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS -- never required, and never forbidden either, for PASS or FAIL (dry-run confirmed both directions: a bare {'verdict':'PASS'} and a bare {'verdict':'FAIL'} validate with no operator_approval; a bare {'verdict':'PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS'} is rejected citing the missing property; {'verdict':'PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS','operator_approval':{}} is separately rejected citing operator_approval's own missing sub-fields). $schema draft 2020-12, $id, title, description declared per schema/README.md's own stated conventions; exemplars read and matched: schema/qa_verdict.schema.json (enum + required-array house pattern) and schema/contract_review.schema.json (single flat object, additionalProperties:true at every level).
design_notes.dry_run_proof: Every mechanism below was proven against SCRATCH COPIES ONLY (a disposable working directory outside the repository tree) before being proposed as a contract verification command; nothing under schema/, tools/, ecosystem/, or any other repo-tree path was created, edited, or deleted to produce this proof. (1) The scratch schema self-validates as a Draft202012 schema (jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema) and its json.dumps contains all three verdict tokens -- the literal feature_list_005.json acceptance-test one-liner, run verbatim against the scratch copy. (2) Three scratch positive fixtures (bare PASS, bare FAIL, and PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS-with-a-complete-operator_approval-and-non-empty-exceptions) each validate with zero errors. (3) Two scratch negative fixtures -- one with verdict 'PASS_WITH_WARNING' (unsupported value) and one with verdict PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS but no 'operator_approval' key at all -- each independently raise jsonschema.ValidationError. (4) The literal feature_list_005.json glob-based negative-fixture acceptance-test one-liner (glob 'tools/fixtures/*preflight*', filtered to 'invalid'/'neg' substrings, validate neg[0], expect ValidationError) was run against the scratch fixtures directory and returned exit 0 (both matching candidates independently confirmed to fail validation, so the result is correct regardless of glob ordering -- non-vacuous by construction, not by picking a favorable glob order). (5) Three additional DIRECT schema-level assertions (not fixture-dependent) were dry-run: an inline {'verdict':'BOGUS'} instance is rejected; an inline bare {'verdict':'PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS'} instance is rejected citing the missing 'operator_approval'; inline bare {'verdict':'PASS'} and {'verdict':'FAIL'} instances both validate with no operator_approval demanded -- proving the conditional is scoped to exactly the one verdict value, neither over- nor under-constraining the other two. (6) The planned NIZAM.json edit (append 'schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json' to both the top-level 'schemas' array and the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array) was applied to a scratch copy of the REAL NIZAM.json and (a) still validates against registry/nizam-index.schema.json, and (b) a walker mirroring tools/validate.sh's check_c4_index logic, run against the REAL repository tree as it exists today (schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json not yet created), correctly reports the new path as missing/would-FAIL -- and, when the same schema file is copied into a scratch-local schema/ directory alongside a scratch mini-index, the identical walker logic correctly reports would-PASS. This proves both directions: the acceptance test is not already trivially true today (grep for the string in the REAL NIZAM.json today returns not-found, confirmed live), and the check_c4_index mechanism itself is sound (fails on a dangling index entry, passes once the referenced file exists). (7) The planned schema/README.md edit (new Schemas-table row, frontmatter version 0.4.0->0.5.0, one new change_log entry) was applied to a scratch copy of the REAL schema/README.md and: the frontmatter still validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json; the new change_log entry's version ('0.5.0') matches the bumped frontmatter version (satisfying tools/validate.sh's check_c8_version_changelog, which only requires a change_log entry OR a CHANGELOG.md mention -- the former is used here, consistent with feature 001's schema/README.md precedent and never touching CHANGELOG.md, which phase 005 defers entirely to the release feature 045 per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.1); and tools/verify_lib.sh's own vlib_section_grep, run unmodified against both the scratch 'before' and 'after' copies anchored to '^## Schemas$', correctly reports absent-before and present-after (anti-vacuity, both directions).
design_notes.concurrency_fence: Feature 034 (Immutable Baseline Protocol) is running CONCURRENTLY in this same working tree (confirmed live: .agent/contracts/034.json already exists, untracked, at the time this contract was drafted). Its contracted surfaces are ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md (new), a one-line ecosystem/README.md nav-annotation flip, .agent/contracts/034.json, .agent/evidence/034*, and .agent/qa/034*. This feature's scope-guard (verification entry 26, renumbered from 24 in revision 1) allow-lists ecosystem/README.md and ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md SOLELY to tolerate 034's concurrent legitimate edits without a false-positive scope-guard failure; this feature's own files_create/files_modify below deliberately OMIT both paths -- this feature does not create, edit, or delete either of them under any circumstance. Because 034 legitimately changes ecosystem/README.md and creates ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md during this same window, this feature's ecosystem-scoped non-goal checks (verification entries 20-21, renumbered from 18-19) are deliberately narrowed to NEVER assert anything about those two paths' contents or existence: the forbidden-creation per-file loop (entry 20) enumerates only the SEVEN other not-yet-existing protocol documents (00, 03-08) that neither 034 nor this feature is authorized to create, and the byte-identity check (entry 21) covers only ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md (033's already-shipped, unrelated-to-034 file). The .agent/ immutability guard (verification entry 25, renumbered from 23) likewise excludes both this feature's own 038.json/038*.json artifacts AND feature 034's concurrently-authored 034.json/034*.json artifacts, mirroring contract 033's own precedent for tolerating a different in-flight feature's concurrent audit artifacts without treating them as historical/immutable.
design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts: This is a CONTRACT-PROPOSAL step (status=proposed): no implementation edits, no git commit, and no .agent/run_state.json rewrite happen under this document -- run_state.json is orchestrator-owned and batched centrally. Nothing under schema/, tools/, ecosystem/, or any other repo-tree surface was created, edited, or deleted to produce this proposal or its dry-run proofs; every proof ran against disposable scratch copies outside the repository.
design_notes.rev1_summary: REVISION 1 of 3 (per .agent/qa/038-contract-review.json, revision_reviewed=0, REJECTED, single HIGH finding, non_blocking observations otherwise). Gap: 23 of 24 verification entries were sound and independently re-proven by the evaluator, but nothing in the suite proved operator_approval's CONTENT must be structurally complete once the key is present -- the evaluator built a disposable LOOSE scratch schema variant (operator_approval typed but carrying no internal 'required' array of its own) alongside the STRICT variant this contract's own design_notes.schema_design already described in prose, and both variants passed all 24 entries identically; a probe instance {'verdict':'PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS','exceptions':[...],'operator_approval':{}} validated under the loose variant and was rejected only under the strict one. FIX in this revision: (1) design_notes.schema_design now commits explicitly to operator_approval declaring its OWN internal 'required':['approver','approved_at','authorization'] plus 'minLength':1 on each sub-field, not merely the outer conditional's coarser 'operator_approval' key-presence check; (2) two new direct verification entries added (now array positions 7 and 8, immediately after the existing verdict-enum-scoping cluster at 4-6, mirroring that same rigor): entry 7 proves {'operator_approval':{}} (empty object, key present) is rejected; entry 8 proves each of the three sub-fields, independently either MISSING or present-as-an-empty-string (six probe instances total), is rejected; (3) a THIRD negative fixture, tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_invalid_approval_incomplete.json (operator_approval:{}, the exact evaluator gap-probe shape), added to files_create and wired into the renumbered 'all three named negative fixtures' entry (now position 12, extended from 'both' to 'all three'); (4) evidence_convention's stated entry count updated 24 -> 26, and every design_notes/verification-entry cross-reference renumbered to match the two new insertions (entries 7-8 pushed everything from the former entry 7 onward down by two positions). Re-proven in this revision, scratch-only, both directions: a LOOSE variant (operator_approval's internal 'required' removed) FAILS the new entry 7 and entry 8 missing-sub-field probes exactly as the evaluator's gap-probe predicted; the STRICT, as-designed schema (which already carried the internal 'required' and 'minLength' this contract's original prose described, even though nothing tested it) PASSES both new entries and both new/extended fixture checks. estimated_lines revised 215 -> 231 to reflect the added fixture (+12 lines) and the two new verification-entry command bodies (+lines of contract JSON itself, immaterial to the repo diff this contract authorizes).

files_create.purpose: JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) for the clean-state preflight run's verdict artifact: verdict enum exactly [PASS, PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS, FAIL], and an if/then conditional requiring a structured operator_approval object (approver, approved_at, authorization) plus a non-empty exceptions array whenever verdict is PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS.
POSITIVE fixture: a bare PASS verdict, no exceptions or operator_approval needed.
POSITIVE fixture: a bare FAIL verdict (with an illustrative blocking finding), no operator_approval needed -- proves the third verdict value is also schema-valid on its own, not only PASS.

POSITIVE fixture: PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS with a complete, valid operator_approval object (approver, approved_at, authorization) and a non-empty exceptions array -- proves the conditional's happy path, not only its rejection path.
NEGATIVE fixture (handover F-008 minimum case 1): verdict 'PASS_WITH_WARNING', an unsupported value outside the three-value enum -- must be rejected.
NEGATIVE fixture (handover F-008 minimum case 2): verdict PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS with an exceptions array but NO operator_approval key at all -- must be rejected.

NEGATIVE fixture (added in revision 1 per .agent/qa/038-contract-review.json's HIGH finding): verdict PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS with an exceptions array AND an operator_approval key that IS present but is an empty object ({}) -- zero approver identity, timestamp, or authorization recorded. Distinct from preflight_verdict_invalid_exceptions.json (key absent entirely): this fixture proves the schema rejects a present-but-substantively-incomplete approval, closing the exact gap the evaluator's loose-vs-strict scratch-schema probe reproduced.

files_modify.purpose: Index the new schema: append 'schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json' to both the top-level 'schemas' array and the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array (mirroring every one of the 11 pre-existing schemas, which already appear in both lists).

Add a Schemas-table row for preflight_verdict.schema.json; bump frontmatter version 0.4.0 -> 0.5.0 with a matching change_log entry, per the house pattern the 0.3.0 (contract_review.schema.json) and 0.4.0 (work-packet.schema.json) entries already established in this same file.

non_goals: No edit to ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md, ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md, ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md, ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md, ecosystem/05_release_train_coordination.md, ecosystem/06_simplification_review.md, ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md, or ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md -- protocol prose is out of this feature's scope (033/034/035/036 and successor-phase-deferred protocols own it, per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3)

No edit to, or assertion about, ecosystem/README.md or ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md -- both are feature 034's concurrently in-flight scope (see design_notes.concurrency_fence); this feature's scope-guard tolerates 034's concurrent edits to those two paths without authorizing this feature to make any of its own, and this feature's non-goal checks deliberately do not sweep either path

No creation or edit of schema/engineering_finding.schema.json, schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, or any other schema/*.json file besides schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json -- the finding/baseline schemas are features 039/037's scope; this schema's 'exceptions' array items are deliberately open-shaped so they compose with, rather than pre-empt, 039's finding schema

No creation or edit of tools/ecosystem_preflight.py or any other file under tools/ besides the six new tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_*.json fixtures -- deterministic preflight tooling (the CLI that actually PRODUCES a verdict artifact) is feature 041's scope; this feature only defines the artifact's shape and disposable fixtures for it
No edit to tools/skill.json, tools/SKILL.md, or tools/interface.md -- capability routing is feature 040's scope

No modification of tools/validate.sh or tools/verify_lib.sh -- this feature composes the existing, unmodified vlib_section_grep and vlib_scope_guard primitives and does not add a new --target routing discriminator for preflight-verdict JSON fixtures (that routing extension, if ever wanted, is out of scope here and not required by any acceptance test)

No edit to CHANGELOG.md -- CHANGELOG consolidation for phase 005 is the release feature's (045, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) responsibility per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.1; this feature records its change solely via schema/README.md's own frontmatter change_log, per the same file's own 0.3.0/0.4.0 precedent
No edit to docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, or docs/planning/DEBT.md -- no planning-artifact or debt changes are authorized by this feature

No edit to .agent/run_state.json, any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 038.json, any .agent/qa/*.json, or any prior feature_list_00N.json -- those are immutable audit records or owned by the orchestrator

No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md -- tolerated in the scope-guard allow-list solely to avoid a false-positive scope-guard failure, not because this feature is authorized to modify it
No git commit of any kind is performed under this contract or its implementation step -- the deliverable is verified in the working tree; committing is a separate, later pipeline action
No execution of any outstanding phase-005 human gate (H-FRAMEWORK-SCOPE, H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, H-RISK) -- this feature's operator_gate is false

verification.acceptance_test: F-008/AT: the schema file exists.
verification.command: test -f schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json && echo PASS || { echo "schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json missing"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity master proof for the schema, mirroring the 033/031 fixture-probe precedent): schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json does not exist at HEAD, so every content-anchored check on it below is proven non-vacuous by construction.
verification.command: ! git show HEAD:schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo PASS || { echo "schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json already exists at HEAD -- content checks below would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-008/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): the schema is itself a valid JSON Schema and constrains the verdict to the three values.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; s=json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json')); jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(s); assert 'PASS' in json.dumps(s) and 'PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS' in json.dumps(s) and 'FAIL' in json.dumps(s)" && echo PASS || { echo "schema invalid or missing a verdict token"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (direct, non-fixture-dependent proof of 'Three verdicts supported' / exactly-three enum): an inline instance with an unsupported verdict value is rejected by the schema itself.

ADDITIONAL (direct, non-fixture-dependent proof of 'Exceptions require structured approval state'): a bare PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS instance with no operator_approval key is rejected by the schema itself.
ADDITIONAL (proves the conditional is correctly scoped -- PASS and FAIL never demand operator_approval): bare {'verdict':'PASS'} and bare {'verdict':'FAIL'} both validate with zero errors.
verification.command:
python3 -c "import json,jsonschema
s=json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json'))
jsonschema.validate({'verdict':'PASS'}, s)
jsonschema.validate({'verdict':'FAIL'}, s)" && echo PASS || { echo "PASS or FAIL incorrectly required operator_approval"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (added in revision 1, per .agent/qa/038-contract-review.json HIGH finding -- direct, non-fixture-dependent proof that 'structured approval state' means operator_approval's CONTENT, not merely its key-presence): an instance with verdict PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS, a non-empty exceptions array, and operator_approval PRESENT but an EMPTY object ({}) is rejected by the schema itself.

ADDITIONAL (added in revision 1, per .agent/qa/038-contract-review.json HIGH finding, mirroring entries 4-6's rigor): each of operator_approval's three sub-fields (approver, approved_at, authorization), independently either MISSING from an otherwise-complete operator_approval, or present as an EMPTY STRING, is rejected -- six probe instances in total, none of which may validate.
F-008/AT: all three POSITIVE fixtures (one per supported verdict) validate against the schema with zero errors.
F-008/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): a fixture with an unsupported verdict is rejected (glob-discovered negative fixture).

ADDITIONAL (all three handover F-008 negative cases independently confirmed rejected -- extended in revision 1 to add the third, incomplete-approval fixture -- not just whichever the glob in the prior entry happens to pick first): the unsupported-verdict fixture, the missing-operator-approval fixture, AND the empty-operator-approval fixture each raise ValidationError by name.
F-008/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): NIZAM.json indexes the new schema and stays C4-green.
verification.command: grep -q 'preflight_verdict.schema.json' NIZAM.json && bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json; test $? -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "NIZAM.json does not index the schema, or --target NIZAM.json failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity for the prior entry, RE-ANCHORED TO HEAD per amendment_1 -- mirrors entry 16's pattern exactly, closing the Loop-2-discovered defect where the original working-tree-anchored form was mutually exclusive with entry 12 once implemented): the HEAD copy of NIZAM.json does NOT contain the string 'preflight_verdict.schema.json'.
verification.command: ! (git show HEAD:NIZAM.json | grep -q 'preflight_verdict.schema.json') && echo PASS || { echo "NIZAM.json already indexes preflight_verdict.schema.json AT HEAD -- entry 12 would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (both NIZAM.json indexing surfaces, not only the top-level 'schemas' array): the 'schema' module's own 'key_documents' array also names the new schema.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('NIZAM.json')); mod=[m for m in d['modules'] if m['path']=='schema'][0]; assert 'schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json' in mod['key_documents']" && echo PASS || { echo "schema module key_documents does not list the new schema"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (schema/README.md indexes the new schema, section-anchored per the module's own 'Schemas' table -- not a vacuous whole-file grep): the '## Schemas' section names preflight_verdict.schema.json.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep schema/README.md '^## Schemas$' 'preflight_verdict\.schema\.json' && echo PASS || { echo "schema/README.md Schemas table missing the new row"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity for the prior entry): the HEAD copy of schema/README.md's '## Schemas' section does NOT yet name preflight_verdict.schema.json.
verification.command: t=$(mktemp); git show HEAD:schema/README.md > "${t}"; source tools/verify_lib.sh; if vlib_section_grep "${t}" '^## Schemas$' 'preflight_verdict\.schema\.json'; then echo "HEAD copy already names the schema -- prior entry would be vacuous"; rm -f "${t}"; exit 1; else echo PASS; rm -f "${t}"; fi

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (schema/README.md version-bump discipline, NDS Sec 7 / tools/validate.sh C8): the frontmatter version strictly increased over HEAD.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_version_increased schema/README.md && echo PASS || { echo "schema/README.md version did not strictly increase"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL: schema/README.md's frontmatter change_log includes an entry whose version equals the bumped frontmatter version (satisfies C8 without touching CHANGELOG.md, per the non-goal above).
verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml; d=open('schema/README.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; fm=yaml.safe_load(d); v=fm['version']; assert any(str(e.get('version'))==v for e in (fm.get('change_log') or []))" && echo PASS || { echo "no matching change_log entry for the bumped version"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-008/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): compliance validator stays green.
verification.command: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "validate.sh SUMMARY regressed from 11 passed, 0 failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, per-file existence loop -- never an ls-based multi-argument check, per contract 033's rev1 false-pass defect and fix): none of the seven OTHER not-yet-existing ecosystem protocol documents (00, 03-08) was created; ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md and ecosystem/README.md are deliberately excluded from this loop because feature 034 legitimately creates/edits them concurrently (see design_notes.concurrency_fence).
verification.command: bad=0; for f in ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md ecosystem/05_release_train_coordination.md ecosystem/06_simplification_review.md ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md; do if [ -e "$f" ]; then echo "out-of-scope ecosystem protocol document created: $f"; bad=1; fi; done; if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md (033's already-shipped protocol, unrelated to feature 034) is byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md was modified out of scope"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): no schema/*.json file besides this feature's own new schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json and its authorized schema/README.md edit was modified.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- schema/ ':(exclude)schema/README.md' && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope schema/*.json file was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- schema/ ':(exclude)schema/README.md'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): no pre-existing file under tools/ (validate.sh, verify_lib.sh, skill.json, interface.md, or any existing fixture) was modified -- this feature only ADDS new untracked fixtures, which git diff never reports.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- tools/ && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope tools/ file was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- tools/; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, and docs/planning/DEBT.md are byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- docs/planning/manifest.json docs/planning/ROADMAP.md docs/planning/DEBT.md && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope planning document was modified"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (.agent/ immutability guard, RE-SHAPED per amendment_2 to assert its actual two-part invariant directly, closing the coverage gap the original single allow-list-based form hit once sibling phase-005 contract drafts 035/036 legitimately appeared mid-implementation): (a) no TRACKED/HISTORICAL .agent/qa or .agent/contracts record was modified (git diff --quiet HEAD, not an allow-list); (b) every UNTRACKED addition under .agent/qa or .agent/contracts is confined to a legitimately in-flight phase-005 feature (034-045) or its review artifacts -- any untracked file matching the already-committed-history range (031-033) or any other name is rejected.
verification.command: bad=0; if ! git diff --quiet HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts; then echo "tracked/historical .agent/qa or .agent/contracts record modified:"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts; bad=1; fi; while IFS= read -r line; do case "$line" in "?? "*) path="${line:3}"; case "$path" in .agent/contracts/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/contracts/04[0-5]*.json|.agent/qa/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/qa/04[0-5]*.json) ;; *) echo "out-of-scope untracked .agent/qa or .agent/contracts artifact: $path"; bad=1 ;; esac ;; esac; done < <(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts); if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (scope guard, untracked-aware, concurrency-tolerant per design_notes.concurrency_fence -- ecosystem/README.md and ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md are 034's concurrently in-flight scope, and v0.6.0-release-notes.md is a pre-existing untracked artifact this feature does not touch): only this feature's own new/modified files, plus the tolerated concurrent/pre-existing paths, changed outside .agent/.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json' 'schema/README.md' 'NIZAM.json' 'tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_pass.json' 'tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_fail.json' 'tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_pass_with_exceptions.json' 'tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_invalid_verdict.json' 'tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_invalid_exceptions.json' 'tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_invalid_approval_incomplete.json' 'ecosystem/README.md' 'ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/038-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/038-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/038-verify-26.txt for the twenty-sixth -- the 'verification' array has exactly 26 entries, revised from 24 in revision 1 per .agent/qa/038-contract-review.json). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
